  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section contains a materially unstable contribution claim: paragraph 1 names the role-dialogue protocol as the 'central contribution' and 'principal scientific advance,' while the final paragraph reassigns primacy to the dataset/evaluation harness as the 'principal empirical contribution.' This thesis drift must be surfaced regardless of review type.",
    "R1.2": "A direct load-bearing contradiction exists between paragraphs 2 and 3: paragraph 2 posits 'a central orchestrator agent' that 'arbitrates conflicting proposals' and 'issues the final action token,' while paragraph 3 states the system operates on 'a fully decentralized peer-to-peer channel with no central coordinator.' These descriptions are mutually exclusive and cannot both be accurate; failure to resolve this invalidates the architectural account.",
    "R1.3": "Generalization is claimed 'across embodiments and task families without any task-specific training,' but the sole evaluation platform is a single dual-arm Franka performing tabletop pick-and-place. Additionally, matching the baseline's success rate is framed as a positive result without acknowledging it constitutes a null finding on the primary metric.",
    "R1.6": "The delegation 'Review this section' is compatible with an editorial mandate, which would make prose clarity and paragraph-level quality load-bearing. This ambiguity elevates R1.6 to central pending clarification."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review focus primarily on scientific and technical accuracy (logical consistency, claim-evidence gaps, contribution framing), or on editorial quality (prose clarity, sentence-level writing polish)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section contains a materially unstable contribution claim: paragraph 1 names the role-dialogue protocol as the 'central contribution' and 'principal scientific advance,' while the final paragraph reassigns primacy to the dataset/evaluation harness as the 'principal empirical contribution.' This thesis drift must be surfaced regardless of review type.
rationale.R1.2: A direct load-bearing contradiction exists between paragraphs 2 and 3: paragraph 2 posits 'a central orchestrator agent' that 'arbitrates conflicting proposals' and 'issues the final action token,' while paragraph 3 states the system operates on 'a fully decentralized peer-to-peer channel with no central coordinator.' These descriptions are mutually exclusive and cannot both be accurate; failure to resolve this invalidates the architectural account.
rationale.R1.3: Generalization is claimed 'across embodiments and task families without any task-specific training,' but the sole evaluation platform is a single dual-arm Franka performing tabletop pick-and-place. Additionally, matching the baseline's success rate is framed as a positive result without acknowledging it constitutes a null finding on the primary metric.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Review this section' is compatible with an editorial mandate, which would make prose clarity and paragraph-level quality load-bearing. This ambiguity elevates R1.6 to central pending clarification.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on scientific and technical accuracy (logical consistency, claim-evidence gaps, contribution framing), or on editorial quality (prose clarity, sentence-level writing polish)?
